content: To validate npm package publish timestamps in bash: use curl to fetch the package's full registry document from registry.npmjs.org/<scope>/<pkg> (or registry.npmjs.org/<pkg>), then parse the '.time.<version>' field with jq. Example: PUBLISH_DATE=$(curl -s 'https://registry.npmjs.org/@opengsd/gsd-core' | jq -r '.time["1.4.3"]'). Compare dates as ISO-8601 strings (lexicographic comparison works for UTC timestamps). For govulncheck: use 'https://proxy.golang.org/golang.org/x/vuln/@v/v1.3.0.info' and parse the '.Time' field with jq. The verifier script: (1) reads versions.lock JSON with jq, (2) for each package, fetches publish timestamp from registry, (3) compares timestamp <= cooldown_date+'T23:59:59Z', (4) exits 1 if any violation. For npm registry, the main package document (not version-specific endpoint) contains the time field.
